• Led 4 person team building RAG based extraction system using Azure OpenAI and LangChain, achieving 85% extraction accuracy on 200 lease agreements and amendment documents • Engineered prompt optimization framework reducing LLM hallucination rate by 30% through structured output validation, semantic chunking strategies, and retrieval augmented generation with vector similarity search
...
“I had the pleasure of working with Shubham during his year-long data science internship at Globe Life. Shubham took a lot of initiative and showcased impressive technical prowess across multiple projects; a significant contribution involved automating our agent inventory reporting with a very low margin of error, which effectively cut down significant manual hours for our team and freed up time for more strategic work.
 
He was also involved in building several ML models for our mailing campaigns using AWS Sagemaker & S3; he excelled at understanding the lifecycle of a ML campaign from conception to execution. His work encompassed data preprocessing, feature engineering, model development, and deployment, all of which he became proficient with on our AWS infrastructure. His contributions to our python modules regarding the same were thorough, clearly documented and of high-quality.
 
Beyond his technical abilities, he was a great team player with excellent communication and required minimal supervision with tasks. He was proactive in seeking feedback, collaborative with team members, keen on identifying process improvements and always willing to take on challenging tasks. His positive attitude and professional demeanor made him a pleasure to work with.
 
I would strongly recommend him for any data science or ML engineering role!”
...
From a technical standpoint, Shubham worked across a diverse range of AI and machine learning projects. He worked on LLM-powered test automation framework using LangChain and LLMs, optimized computer vision models using YOLOv5 for UI element detection, and developed NLP solutions including BERT-based sentiment classifiers and semantic similarity models using sentence transformers. He also implemented explainability frameworks using SHAP to help stakeholders understand model predictions. His versatility across multiple projects from generative AI to computer vision to NLP was truly impressive.
...
I’m excited to share that I’ll be joining Globe Life as a Data Science Intern starting next week!

results.title: Robert Stewart
highlights:
Results-oriented Software Engineer and Automation Architect with over 13 years of progressive experience within the Globe Life ecosystem. I currently operate as an RPA Senior Developer, architecting enterprise-level automation solutions for the Data Analytics and AI department. My technical expertise centers on bridging the gap between legacy operations and modern software solutions. I specialize in engineering complex SQL ETL pipelines, developing custom C# automation tools, and implementing Robotic Process Automation (RPA) to safeguard critical revenue streams. I leverage technologies such as C#,.NET, SQL Server Integration Services (SSIS), Python, and CI/CD workflows to build innovative solutions that streamline business logic and eliminate manual processing. I hold a Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science Software Engineering from Colorado Technical University (Summa Cum Laude). I am currently completing my Master's degree in
...
same field, with an anticipated graduation date of June 2026. Committed to continuous professional growth and lifelong learning, I am dedicated to applying advanced engineering skills to drive significant business value. I am also a proud Member of the National Society of Collegiate Scholars (NSCS).
...
Contracted through eBusiness Technologies to engineer automated business solutions for Globe Life. Currently directing the transition from traditional RPA platforms to custom, AI-driven automation using Python and AWS cloud services. Key Responsibilities: Build proof-of-concept AI Agents using AWS Bedrock and AWS Lambda, replacing legacy vendor-locked RPA workflows with custom, autonomous Python applications. Translate business use cases from Jira user stories into production-ready code, managing the full software development lifecycle from initial architecture to testing and final deployment. Architect and maintain the enterprise RPA Automated Monitoring System (AMS) using Python, Amazon Redshift, and Exchange APIs to evaluate production bot health and trigger targeted failure alerts. Manage enterprise bot deployments, version control, and scheduling for the existing Automation Anywhere 360 infrastructure. Ensure all code and automated workflows comply with internal security protocols, audit trails, and strict insurance regulatory standards.
...
- a critical internal application that centralized document regeneration and workflow management. Business Continuity: Primary point of contact for after-hours ETL recovery, directly safeguarding revenue streams by maintaining uptime for critical customer billing cycles.

results.title: Globe Life Innovation & Technology Culture 2026 | Built In Los Angeles
highlights:
We're Hiring!
...
# Globe Life Innovation & Technology Culture
...
## What is the technology culture like at Globe Life?
...
Globe Life's innovation and product culture is focused on continuously improving the customer experience, modernizing operations, and investing in technology that helps the company serve policyholders more effectively. While Globe Life is rooted in the stability and long-term perspective of the insurance industry, the company continues to invest in digital transformation, data-driven decision-making, process improvement, and technology modernization initiatives that support both customers and employees.
...
Bottom line: Globe Life's innovation and product culture is best described as customer-focused, improvement-oriented, and grounded in long-term thinking. Employees who thrive at Globe Life enjoy finding better ways to serve customers, improve business processes, and contribute to a company that continues to modernize while maintaining the stability and mission-driven focus of a leading insurance provider.
...
## Globe Life's Candidate Tradeoffs
...
- Globe Life places greater emphasis on high-impact innovation within established systems than on unconstrained experimentation.
...
Process Innovation: Feedback suggests Globe Life has modernized core distribution and underwriting workflows through online simplified‑issue applications, e‑applications, and steady systems upgrades that improve conversion and efficiency. The company also supports post‑sale self‑service via an eService portal and mobile apps, indicating sustained operational streamlining.
...
Emerging Technology Adoption: Feedback suggests a tech‑oriented tuck‑in (Evry Health) and ongoing cloud/data hiring and platform investments reflect willingness to adopt newer tools and analytics. Disclosures referencing continued technology and automation efforts signal gradual modernization of back‑end platforms and decisioning.
...
## Globe Life's Tech Stack

results.title: Career Opportunities: Data Science AI Engineer, Advisor (36063) | FlexPilot
highlights:
Career Opportunities: Data Science AI Engineer, Advisor (36063) | FlexPilot
...
# Career Opportunities: Data Science AI Engineer, Advisor (36063)
...
Gainwell Technologies is seeking a highly skilled AI Engineer to design, develop, and deploy advanced AI solutions, including Generative AI (GenAI), across our healthcare technology platforms. This role involves building and optimizing AI technologies, integrating them into existing systems, and ensuring their effectiveness in improving healthcare outcomes and operational efficiency while maintaining compliance with industry standards.
...
- AI Model Development – Design and build AI Solutions that use machine learning models, including GenAI, NLP, and predictive analytics solutions for healthcare applications.
...
- End-to-End AI Solution Deployment – Develop, test, and deploy AI solutions in cloud and on-premise environments, ensuring reliability, scalability, and real-world impact.
...
- Data Engineering & Processing – Work with large healthcare datasets, performing data preprocessing, feature engineering, and model training while ensuring compliance with HIPAA and other regulatory standards.
...
- System Integration – Implement and optimize AI solutions within Gainwell’s existing technology stack, collaborating with engineering teams to ensure seamless integration.
...
- Performance Optimization – Continuously monitor, refine, and optimize AI solutions for accuracy, efficiency, and speed, leveraging MLOps best practices.
...
- AI Research & Innovation – Stay updated with the latest AI/ML advancements, exploring new technologies and methodologies to enhance solution effectiveness.
...
- Master’s or Ph.D in Computer Science, AI, Data Science, or a related field.
...
- 5+ years of experience in AI/ML engineering, with a focus on developing and deploying AI solutions.
...
- Hands-on expertise in production operations involving machine learning, deep learning and GenAI.
...
- Strong programming skills in Python, AI Agent Frameworks (Langchain, Crew.ai), TensorFlow, PyTorch, and other AI frameworks.
...
- Experience developing, deploying and finetuning LLMs (GPT, Gemini, Claude or similar) for real world applications including prompt engineering, model optimization, and inference efficiency.
...
- Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) and MLOps for scalable AI deployments.
...
- Proficiency in working with big data technologies (Spark, Hadoop, SQL, NoSQL databases).
...
- Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to translate business challenges into AI-driven solutions.
...
- Knowledge of healthcare AI applications and regulatory compliance (HIPAA, CMS) is a plus.
...
- Fully Remote Opportunity – Work from anywhere in the U.S.
- Minimal Travel Required – Occasional travel opportunities (0-10%).
- Opportunity to Work on Cutting-Edge AI Solutions in a mission-driven healthcare technology environment.

results.title: Associate Applied AI Engineer – GenAI Systems | Hirenixa
highlights:
AI Engineer – GenAI Systems

Remote, USA

Posted Jun 17, 2026

Full-time

 Manulife is making a significant investment in Advanced Analytics and GenAI to transform how Finance, Treasury, and Actuarial teams make decisions. The Associate Applied AI Engineer will help deliver AI and GenAI capabilities that integrate effectively into real business workflows by translating business challenges into structured solutions and collaborating closely with various teams. Responsibilities Contribute to end-to-end solution design (GenAI + ML) Translate business problems into a clear solution approach, including user workflow, data flow, model approach, evaluation plan, and operational controls Create lightweight, high-quality design artifacts such as system context, runtime sequence, agent/tool maps, data lineage, and decision logs that support implementation and governance Participate in design discussions and make thoughtful trade-offs across accuracy, explainability, cost, latency, and maintainability Build models and GenAI components for Finance & Actuarial use cases Develop ML solutions such as forecasting, classification, NLP, anomaly detection, optimization, and scenario analysis Build GenAI capabilities such as retrieval-based solutions (RAG), structured summarization and extraction, transaction understanding, variance explanation, and tool-using workflows where appropriate Engineer features from structured and unstructured data and help ensure solutions remain robust as data evolves Apply strong evaluation and testing practices Implement performance evaluation using holdouts, backtesting, error analysis, and fit-for-purpose metrics aligned to the business problem For GenAI, help design practical evaluation approaches such as scenario coverage, edge cases, human review rubrics, quality scoring, and regression testing Document model limitations clearly and support guardrails that improve the reliability and safe use of outputs Partner closely to productionize and operate solutions Collaborate with Data Engineering, ML Engineering, and Software teams to productionize solutions through reliable data pipelines, model packaging, CI/CD, deployment, and monitoring Write maintainable, tested code using strong software engineering practices such as version control, modular design, logging, and code review Support monitoring for data quality, drift, performance deterioration, and operational failures, and help investigate issues when thresholds are breached Contribute to runbooks and support adoption and UAT with business users Work in a governed environment Contribute to the documentation and evidence required for model risk review, including assumptions, validation results, monitoring plans, UAT evidence, and approvals Ensure privacy and security expectations are met through data minimization, appropriate access controls, and safe handling of sensitive information Follow established standards for reproducibility, traceability, model documentation, and auditability Grow team capability and delivery maturity Learn quickly from design reviews, code reviews, and stakeholder feedback, and apply those lessons to future work Contribute reusable components, templates, examples, and testing patterns that make team delivery faster and more consistent Stay current with emerging AI and GenAI engineering patterns and bring forward practical ideas that improve how the team builds solutions Skills Master's or PhD in Computer Science, Statistics, Machine Learning, Applied Mathematics, Operations Research, Engineering, or a related quantitative field 0–3 years of experience in applied data science / machine learning, including internships, co-ops, research, or early-career industry experience; strong academic project work may also be considered Strong Python and SQL, with solid software engineering fundamentals such as Git-based workflows, code reviews, unit and integration testing, logging, readable code structure, debugging, and basic performance

results.title: Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist) | Wfh
highlights:
Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist) | Wfh
...
# Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist)
...
Join the core team at Eclipse, where we’re building an AI agent-first marketplace that connects intelligence with real-world tasks, starting with data collection and labeling. We are seeking a Data Scientist to establish the foundation for how our data is labeled, processed, and prepared for consumption by next-generation Large Language Models (LLMs). Your work will be critical in transforming our raw data collections into valuable, AI-ready datasets.
...
- Proven experience as a Data Scientist or Machine Learning Engineer with a focus on data quality and preparation.
- Strong understanding of data labeling methodologies and hands-on experience with data annotation platforms and workflows.
...
- Demonstrated experience preparing datasets for training and fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs), including knowledge of techniques like tokenization, embeddings, and NER.
...
- Proficiency in Python and common data science libraries (e.g., Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, spaCy, Hugging Face).
...
- Experience using APIs/SDKs to automate data annotation and active learning loops.
...
- Responsibilities
...
- Develop Data Labeling Strategies: Design and document a formal data annotation strategy, including clear, scalable, and efficient guidelines for labeling our data. Define and enforce quality metrics, including inter-annotator agreement.
...
- Optimize for LLM Consumption: Research, define, and prototype the optimal data formats, structures, and pre-processing steps required for fine-tuning and training LLMs on our datasets.
...
- Data Quality Analysis: Establish automated processes and metrics to analyze the quality of both raw and labeled data, providing feedback to improve our data collection and labeling workflows.
...
- Collaborate with Engineering: Work closely with the engineering team to guide the implementation of data processing pipelines and ensure the data infrastructure meets the needs of ML applications.
...
- Nice-to-Haves
- Experience with audio data processing and relevant libraries.
- Familiarity with data annotation platforms and tools.
- Knowledge of modern MLOps principles and practices.
- Experience with large language model data curation and Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) pipelines.
